items.title: Putin denounces Nato at scaled back Victory Day parade
items.summary: The Russian leader used his annual speech to justify his so-called special military operation in Ukraine.

items.title: Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure'

items.summary: Some on the Spanish island worry the imminent arrival of the MV Hondius could pose a health risk.

items.title: Hovering objects and flashing lights: what we learned from UFO documents released by the Pentagon
items.summary: The US has published transcripts, video clips and audio recordings about unidentified flying objects, also called unidentified anomalous phenomena.

items.title: Hungary's new PM to be sworn in during 'regime change' party
items.summary: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.

items.title: International cyber attack disrupts swathe of universities and schools
items.summary: A hacking group breached the academic software Canvas, used by thousands of schools and universities across the globe.

items.title: Greece examines mystery naval drone found in Ionian Sea
items.summary: Greek media say the drone, which reportedly had explosives, could be either Ukrainian or linked to the Russia-Ukraine war.

items.title: Australian by-election a litmus test for right-wing One Nation Party
items.summary: Victory for the populist party in Farrer, New South Wales, would see One Nation's first MP elected to Australia's national parliament.

items.title: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
items.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

items.title: White House calls Mark Hamill 'sick' after actor's Trump grave post
items.summary: The Star Wars actor apologised and deleted the post, which contained an AI image of the president in a shallow grave.

items.title: Oil prices rise after US and Iran exchange fire in Hormuz strait
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran is still in place.

items.title: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
items.summary: The Constitutional Court says MPs were was wrong to block impeachment proceedings against Cyril Ramaphosa.

items.title: US jet fuel could be used in Europe to ease possible shortages

items.summary: The Bridesmaids star claims an actress retracted a complaint about sexual harassment by a producer.

items.title: Two Islamic-State linked Australian women charged with crimes against humanity
items.summary: A third woman who also returned to Australia from Syria was charged with joining a terrorist organisation.

items.title: Ethiopian woman's joy at rare quintuplets after 12 years trying for a baby
items.summary: The woman, 35, says she was praying for a baby and was "overjoyed" to be "blessed with five at once".

items.title: World Cup fans in China and India face broadcast uncertainty
items.summary: Broadcasters in the two countries have yet to reach deals with Fifa, just weeks before the tournament is due to start.

items.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

items.title: US to revoke passports of parents with child support debt
items.summary: Parents who have outstanding debt of more than $2,500 (€1,844) in child support payments could be impacted.

items.summary: From dancefloor bangers to heartfelt ballads, here are the highlights and low points of Eurovision.

items.summary: A former ally of Mamata Banerjee, the BJP leader will be West Bengal’s next chief minister.

items.title: Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
items.summary: About 12 countries have so far been linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius, as the UN health agency confirms at least five cases.

items.summary: The US and Brazilian presidents did not appear together publicly, but traded compliments after their talks.

items.summary: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.

items.summary: Leo, the first American pontiff, drew Trump's ire after becoming a firm critic of the war and the administration's hardline immigration policies.
